title: db.rs balance() SUM(value_zatoshi) can silently overflow SQLite i64 arithmetic
description: db.rs balance(): the SQL query uses SUM(value_zatoshi) without any overflow guard. SQLite INTEGER arithmetic is 64-bit signed; if multiple unspent notes whose combined value exceeds i64::MAX (~9.2e18 zatoshi) are present, SQLite returns NULL or a wrong negative value. The code catches negative values via u64::try_from but the error message says 'notes table may be corrupt' rather than 'arithmetic overflow', masking the actual cause. At realistic ZEC supply (~2.1e15 zatoshi total) this cannot happen with real notes, but a wallet accepting synthetic note values from a test harness or a compromised scanner could trigger it. The SUM should be guarded with a CAST check or the query should use MIN/MAX bounds on individual rows before summing.
close_reason: Not silent: if SUM overflows i64, SQLite returns REAL, sqlx fails to decode as i64, balance() returns Err. Error message says 'corrupt' but the fix is just a doc improvement. Already safe.
